title: Codex Adversarial Review for Lucius Gate
summary: Codex plugin provides /codex:adversarial-review that pressure-tests code. Could serve as Lucius sub-gate, but integration requires Node.js subprocess wrapping.

attack_surfaces_mapped.auth_boundaries: Can focus adversarial review on auth code paths
attack_surfaces_mapped.data_loss_windows: Can review backup/write patterns for gaps
attack_surfaces_mapped.rollback_safety: BEST FIT: review rollback code, git operations, state mutations
attack_surfaces_mapped.degraded_deps: Can check dependency handling under failure

integration_plan.approach: Subprocess wrapper in Python calling Codex CLI
integration_plan.prototype_target: rollback_safety (most relevant to Batcave git operations)

blockers: Codex CLI must be installed on Oracle
recommendation: Prototype rollback_safety gate in Session 36. Install Codex CLI, write thin Python wrapper, test on one PR diff. If successful, wire into pre_commit_check as optional gate.
